name: Should I replace baseboards during flooring installation?

mainEntity.name: Should baseboards be removed or replaced when installing new flooring?
mainEntity.text: Should baseboards be removed or replaced when installing new flooring?

acceptedAnswer.text: Baseboards are commonly removed before flooring installation because it gives the installer more room to create a clean perimeter and maintain the required expansion gap. In many cases, the same baseboards can be reinstalled after the floor goes in. That is often the most cost-effective option if the trim is in good shape. Replacement makes sense when the old baseboards are damaged, warped, painted poorly, or too short to cover the new floor edge after installation. Some homeowners also choose to upgrade trim for a cleaner finished look. If the baseboards stay, the installer may use shoe molding or quarter round instead. Ask whether baseboard removal, reinstallation, caulking, and painting are included in the quote. Those finishing details can change the final cost and appearance more than many homeowners expect. Good trim work often makes the difference between a project that looks installed and one that looks finished.
acceptedAnswer.description: Baseboards are often removed during flooring installation so the new floor can expand properly and the edges look cleaner. They can usually be reinstalled, but replacement may be needed if they are damaged, outdated, or too short after the new floor height is added.
